title: taraxacum laevigatum subsp. calocephalum
intro: Taraxacum laevigatum subsp. calocephalum is a subspecies of the dandelion plant, characterized by its smooth leaves and yellow flowers. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Taraxacum laevigatum subsp. calocephalum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve liver function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
